task_updates.executor_notes: Validated the fix at logic/test level across all seven success criteria. Criteria 1 and 7 are covered by the new render-bounds validation tests: oversized cropped clips now produce identical render bounds and overlay bounds. Criterion 2 is covered by move-state validation across multiple positions using the shared render/display math. Criterion 3 is covered by a resized same-aspect case, and the aspect-ratio lock edit path itself was unchanged by T4. Criterion 4 is covered by the in-bounds regression test showing render bounds and media bounds stay unchanged for normal clips. Criterion 5 is covered by crop expand/contract validation across two crop states. Criterion 6 is covered by an explicit `configToRows` / `rowsToConfig` round-trip test asserting `x` / `y` / `width` / `height` / crop values persist unchanged. Verification commands passed: focused Vitest suite, focused ESLint, and `tsc --noEmit`. Remaining limitation: this is not a browser E2E visual pass because the sandbox blocks that workflow.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes at logic/test level. The round-trip case is covered by the `configToRows` / `rowsToConfig` assertion in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts`, and the normal-sized regression case is covered by the in-bounds clip test in the same file. All seven criteria were exercised through the shared render/display math, with the remaining limitation that browser-level E2E confirmation is still blocked by the sandbox.
